title: explicit should be preferred on single-parameter constructors and conversion operators
description: If you invoked a method with arguments of the wrong type, you would typically expect an error at compile time (if not in the IDE). However, when the expected parameter is a class with a single-argument constructor, the compiler will implicitly pass the method argument to that constructor to implicitly create an object of the correct type for the method invocation. Alternately, if the wrong type has a conversion operator to the correct type, the operator will be called to create an object of the needed type.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: explicit should be preferred on single-parameter constructors and conversion operators.
